### 1. Arch Linux AUR Hit By Another Wave Of Now More Sophisticated Malware Attack

- Source: [phoronix.com](https://www.phoronix.com/news/Arch-Linux-AUR-More-Malware)
- Category: AI Operations / Agent Control
- Published: 2026-06-15T00:00:00.000Z
- Labels: reproducible, publish_public
- Product relevance: none

Source facts:
- Over 400 Arch Linux AUR packages were compromised in a supply chain attack using eBPF rootkit payloads for credential harvesting, rated CVSS 8.7.

Builder/operator takeaway: High-severity supply chain attack with real credential harvesting risk. Any developer using AUR packages needs to audit immediately.

Uncertainty: Exact scope may still be expanding — initial reports said 400, some sources suggest up to 1500 packages across multiple waves.

### 2. Don't trust large context windows

- Source: [garrit.xyz](https://garrit.xyz/posts/2026-05-06-dont-trust-large-context-windows)
- Category: AI Operations / Agent Control
- Published: 2026-06-15T00:00:00.000Z
- Labels: builder_reported, publish_public
- Product relevance: none

Source facts:
- Effective LLM context window performance degrades well before the advertised limit, with useful context around ~100k tokens regardless of the marketed window size.

Builder/operator takeaway: Directly relevant to anyone building agent workflows or using AI coding tools. The smart/dumb zone framing is a practical design heuristic.

Uncertainty: The ~100k figure is approximate and model-dependent. Published May 6 but trending on HN June 14.
